findings.explanation: This bucket is the log_bucket target for module.helmrepo's access logs, but it has no retention_policy block. Without bucket-lock retention, an attacker (or a mistaken tofu destroy) can delete or overwrite access logs, defeating the audit trail the logging block is meant to provide. Deterministic rules check whether a bucket *has* logging configured, but cannot tell that *this* bucket is the log sink and therefore needs immutability — that requires following the cross-resource reference.
findings.remediation: Add `retention_policy { retention_period = 2592000 }` (30 days, or your audit window) to google_storage_bucket.logging. Consider also setting `is_locked = true` once the retention period is validated.
findings.title: Access-log sink bucket has no retention_policy — logs are mutable and deletable

findings.explanation: The module declares var.predefined_acl with a validation set that includes publicRead and publicReadWrite, but google_storage_bucket_acl.bucketacl hardcodes predefined_acl = "private" and never references the variable. A caller setting predefined_acl = "publicRead" gets a silent no-op today; a future maintainer who 'fixes' the wiring would suddenly enable a public-exposure path that bypasses the bucket's public_access_prevention intent. Additionally, the ACL resource can never apply successfully because uniform_bucket_level_access = true is hardcoded on the bucket, making ACLs invalid.
findings.remediation: Remove var.predefined_acl and var.acl, and delete google_storage_bucket_acl.bucket-acl.tf — legacy ACLs are incompatible with the module's hardcoded uniform_bucket_level_access = true.
findings.title: var.predefined_acl accepts publicRead/publicReadWrite but is never wired to any resource

suggested_policies.rationale: google_storage_bucket has 5 existing policies (CMEK, UBLA, logging, versioning, labels) but none checks public_access_prevention. This attribute is GCS's primary org-policy-independent control against public exposure; when set to 'inherited' a bucket can be made public via IAM regardless of UBLA. Both buckets in this scan set it to 'enforced' so no finding fired, but the gap means a bucket omitting the attribute or setting 'inherited' would pass holden today.
suggested_policies.yaml:
id: HLD_PROPOSED_001
name: GCS buckets must enforce public access prevention
principle: network-security
severity: HIGH
rationale: |
  When public_access_prevention is unset or set to "inherited", a GCS bucket
  relies solely on the project/organisation policy constraint to block public
  access. If that org policy is absent or later relaxed, the bucket can be
  made world-readable by adding allUsers to its IAM policy — even with
  uniform_bucket_level_access enabled. Setting public_access_prevention =
  "enforced" at the bucket level guarantees the bucket can never be exposed
  publicly, independent of org-policy drift.
remediation: |
  Set `public_access_prevention = "enforced"` on the `google_storage_bucket`
  resource.
compliance:
  cis_gcp: "5.1"
resources:
  google_storage_bucket:
    path: public_access_prevention
    equals: "enforced"

suggested_policies.rationale: coverage_by_type has no entry for google_storage_bucket_acl. This legacy resource can grant anonymous read or write to an entire bucket via predefined_acl = publicRead / publicReadWrite, bypassing IAM-based checks (HLD_GCP_018/039 only cover the iam_binding/iam_member resources). The check is a simple attribute enum and applies universally.
suggested_policies.yaml:
id: HLD_PROPOSED_002
name: GCS bucket ACLs must not grant public access
principle: network-security
severity: CRITICAL
rationale: |
  The google_storage_bucket_acl resource applies legacy ACLs to a bucket. A
  predefined_acl of publicRead or publicReadWrite makes every object in the
  bucket readable (or writable) by anonymous internet users, with no
  authentication. Because ACLs are a separate access-control surface from
  IAM, holden's existing IAM-binding checks (HLD_GCP_018, HLD_GCP_039) do not
  see this exposure path.
remediation: |
  Remove the `google_storage_bucket_acl` resource and rely on IAM with
  `uniform_bucket_level_access = true`, or set `predefined_acl` to `private`
  or `projectPrivate`.
resources:
  google_storage_bucket_acl:
    path: predefined_acl
    not_one_of:
      - publicRead
      - publicReadWrite
  google_storage_default_object_acl:
    path: predefined_acl
    not_one_of:
      - publicRead
      - publicReadWrite
